name: cadaver
title: cadaver
description:
command-line WebDAV client
cadaver supports file upload, download, on-screen display, in-place editing,
namespace operations (move/copy), collection creation and deletion, property
manipulation, and resource locking.
.
Its operation is similar to the standard BSD ftp(1) client and the Samba
Project's smbclient(1).
.
This package includes GnuTLS (HTTPS) support.
.
WebDAV (Web-based Distributed Authoring and Versioning) is a set of
extensions to the HTTP protocol which allow users to collaboratively edit and
manage files on remote web servers.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cadaver because command-line WebDAV client.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cadaver as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cadaver [-trp[-r file][-p host[:port]]][-V][-h] URL` as documented upstream for cadaver.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cadaver $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for cadaver.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cadaver with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cadaver --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
